parameters.name: active
parameters.label: Active
parameters.description: This check box enables the serial connection.

parameters.name: state
parameters.label: State
parameters.description: The type of input transition to monitor.

parameters.name: port
parameters.label: Port
parameters.description: Selects the COM port that the serial connection will use.

parameters.name: baudrate
parameters.label: Baud Rate
parameters.description: The maximum number of bits of information, including "control" bits, that are transmitted per second. Check your input device's default baud rate and set accordingly.

parameters.name: databits
parameters.label: Data Bits
parameters.description: This parameter sets the number of data bits sent in each. Data bits are transmitted "backwards". Backwards refers to the order of transmission, which is from least significant bit (LSB) to most signif

parameters.name: parity
parameters.label: Parity
parameters.description: This parameter can be set to none, even, or odd. The optional parity bit follows the data bits and is included as a simple means of error checking. Parity bits work by specifying ahead of time whether

parameters.name: stopbits
parameters.label: Stop Bits
parameters.description: The last part of transmission packet consists of 1 or 2 Stop bits. The connection will now wait for the next Start bit.

parameters.name: script
parameters.label: Script
parameters.description: Sequence of scripts corresponding to input channels. These strings are sent out the serial port when the corresponding channel change is detected. For example, 2 is sent to the serial port when the th

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page. See .

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).
